source: Wilson (1963) mantle hotspot framework; positions from GVP / plate tectonic references
about: Mantle hotspots are long-lived upwellings of unusually hot rock from the deep mantle. Tectonic plates drift over them, leaving chains of volcanoes and seamounts while the source stays relatively fixed.

hotspots.name: Hawaii
hotspots.note: Archetype oceanic hotspot; the Pacific plate drifts northwest ~7 cm/yr over the source.

hotspots.name: Yellowstone
hotspots.note: Continental hotspot under the North American plate; caldera-forming eruptions and geothermal activity.

hotspots.name: Iceland
hotspots.note: Hotspot meets a spreading ridge, building a subaerial volcanic plateau.

hotspots.name: Galápagos
hotspots.note: Shallow mantle plume near a spreading center; shields and calderas on young oceanic crust.

hotspots.name: Easter
hotspots.note: Nazca plate hotspot; isolated basaltic volcanism far from the nearest spreading ridge.

hotspots.name: Society
hotspots.note: Pacific plate hotspot producing shield volcanoes and reef-capped islands in French Polynesia.

hotspots.name: Samoa
hotspots.note: Pacific hotspot with young basaltic shields; some volcanism may also track nearby ridge processes.

hotspots.name: Macdonald
hotspots.note: Submarine hotspot volcano south of the Society chain on very young Pacific lithosphere.

hotspots.name: Cobb
hotspots.note: Juan de Fuca / Gorda ridge region; plume magma mixes with spreading-center volcanism.

hotspots.name: Tristan
hotspots.note: South Atlantic hotspot; Walvis Ridge records Africa's passage over the plume.

hotspots.name: Bouvet
hotspots.note: Remote subantarctic hotspot volcano on the Southwest Indian Ridge sector of the Atlantic.

hotspots.name: Réunion
hotspots.note: Active Indian Ocean hotspot; Deccan Traps flood basalts linked to its early history.

hotspots.name: Kerguelen
hotspots.note: Large submarine plateau built by prolonged plume volcanism in the southern Indian Ocean.

hotspots.name: Afar
hotspots.note: Mantle upwelling at a continental rift; flood basalts and persistent spreading-related volcanism.

hotspots.name: Canary
hotspots.note: Atlantic hotspot archipelago off northwest Africa; large ocean-island shield volcanoes.

hotspots.name: Azores
hotspots.note: Hotspot-related magmatism near the Azores triple junction and slow-spreading ridge.

hotspots.name: Ascension
hotspots.note: Small mid-ocean island volcano on young South Atlantic crust far from continents.

hotspots.name: Cape Verde
hotspots.note: Atlantic hotspot chain west of Senegal; alkaline ocean-island volcanism on old oceanic lithosphere.

hotspots.name: Eifel
hotspots.note: Small continental hotspot / lithospheric anomaly; sporadic maar and caldera eruptions.

hotspots.name: Pitcairn
hotspots.note: Pacific plate hotspot trail between the Gambier and Austral island groups.

hotspots.name: Marquesas
hotspots.note: Older Pacific hotspot archipelago with deeply eroded basaltic shields.

hotspots.name: Amsterdam
hotspots.note: Indian Ocean intraplate volcanism on the Antarctic plate near the Southeast Indian Ridge.

hotspots.name: Fernando de Noronha
hotspots.note: South American plate hotspot; alkaline ocean-island volcanism off northeast Brazil.

hotspots.name: New England
hotspots.note: Fossil hotspot track on North Atlantic abyssal plain; active volcanism long since passed.
